files_outside_owned_paths.issue: Parent-level __init__.py files outside W5 owned paths (scripts/mvpplus/, tests/mvpplus/)

file_lock.fix: os.open(O_CREAT | O_EXCL) for atomic exclusive creation
details: O_CREAT | O_EXCL ensures exactly one winner among simultaneous callers
_pid_is_alive() cross-platform check prevents stale recovery when PID is still alive
release() verifies ownership before deleting lock file
Documented stale recovery policy in docstring and code comments

atomic_json.issue: All writers shared one fixed .tmp path per target
atomic_json.fix: Unique temp file per writer (PID + nanosecond + monotonic sequence counter)
details: _unique_tmp_path() produces collision-free distinct paths using PID, time.time_ns() and a monotonic counter
Temp file in same directory for atomic os.replace
_atomic_replace retries on Windows sharing/access-denied errors with exponential back-off

apscheduler.issue: Version pinned to 3.10.4, project policy is 3.11.x
details: Scheduler never started by code - enabled=False is the default

rollback.issue: Destructive DROP TABLE was presented as a default direct action
rollback.fix: Step 3 now requires backup, explicit approval, and dry-run/plan output
details: dry_run_rollback() provides a plan without executing
Step 3 command is a dry-run echo with prerequisites documented
Note explicitly states data-loss risk and 3 prerequisites

path_repair.issue: Parent-level __init__.py files outside W5 owned paths

known_limitations: PID-alive check on Windows falls back to 'assume dead' if ctypes/kernel32 calls fail
File lock race test uses threads (same-process) rather than separate processes; true cross-process race should be tested in integration
Atomic JSON concurrent writers test validates unique temp paths but not the final content - last-writer-wins is by design
